name: Design Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Design Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing GeneTAC molecules, a novel class of small-molecule gene targeted chimera therapeutics for diseases caused by inherited nucleotide repeat expansion mutations. It has programs for Friedreich ataxia, Fuchs endothelial corneal dystrophy, myotonic dystrophy type-1, and Huntington's disease, among others. The company has no approved products and no revenue from product sales.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company pioneering the research and development of GeneTAC molecules, which are a novel class of small-molecule gene targeted chimera therapeutic candidates designed to be disease-modifying by addressing the underlying cause of diseases caused by inherited nucleotide repeat expansion mutations.

Our information technology systems or sensitive data, or those of our third-party CROs or other contractors or consultants, or third parties with whom we work, may fail or suffer security incidents, loss or leakage of data, and other disruptions, which could result in a material disruption of our product candidates' development programs, compromise sensitive data related to our business or prevent us from accessing critical information, potentially exposing us to liability or otherwise adversely affecting our business.

catalysts.event: Provide update from RESTORE-FA Phase 1/2 MAD trial on effect of DT-216P2 on frataxin levels after 12 weeks of dosing
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate providing an update from the RESTORE-FA trial on the effect of DT-216P2 on endogenous frataxin levels following 12 weeks of dosing in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Report data from Phase 2 biomarker trial of DT-168 in FECD
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate reporting data from the Phase 2 biomarker trial in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Begin dosing DM1 patients in Phase 1 MAD trial of DT-818
catalysts.sentence: We plan to begin dosing DM1 patients in a Phase 1 MAD trial in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Report results from DT-818 Phase 1 MAD trial
catalysts.sentence: The study, with results anticipated in 2027, is expected to assess safety and correction of mis-splicing.

tamClaims.claim: Nucleotide repeat expansion diseases affect millions of individuals worldwide
tamClaims.sentence: As a platform, we believe that GeneTAC molecules have broad potential applicability across currently unaddressed degenerative, monogenic nucleotide repeat expansion diseases affecting millions of individuals worldwide.
